rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON PETITIONER'S REQUEST FOR STAY OF SUSPENSION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 7, 2025, Line 5, HEARING ON PETITIONER'S REQUEST FOR STAY OF SUSPENSION. Petitioner Daniel Greenwood's Motion for Temporary Stay of Driver's License Suspension Pending Outcome of Writ of Mandate, submitted upon stipulation of Petitioner and Respondent Steven Gordon, Director of the Department of Motor Vehicles, is GRANTED consistent with the stipulation, including the ID condition, except the duration of the stay is limited to eight months or until further order of the court, whichever occurs first. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: TAMARA LYONS VS. LAKEPORT POST ACUTE, LLC, (CA) ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Response To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions For $3195 Against Plaintiff And Her Counsel, Jointly And Severally
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Tuesday, January 7, 2025, Line 1. DEFENDANT LAKEPORT POST ACUTE, LLC's Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Response To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions For $3195 Against Plaintiff And Her Counsel, Jointly And Severally. Defendant Lakeport Post Acute, LLC's And Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Response To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions For $3195 Against Plaintiff And Her Counsel, Jointly And Severally to which no opposition was filed is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Good cause appearing, the court orders: 1) Plaintiff to serve complete verified responses, without objections, to Defendant's Requests For Production of Documents, Set One, Nos. 1-138 within seven court days of service of notice of this order, and (2) an award for monetary sanctions against Plaintiff and her counsel, jointly and severally, in favor of Defendant in the amount of $2,485. The motion is denied to the extent additional or different relief is requested. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: RAJIV TARUN PARMAR VS. AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Tuesday, January 7, 2025, Line 2. PLAINTIFF RAJIV PARMAR's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two. Plaintiff Rajiv Tarun Parmar's motion to compel further responses to plaintiff's request for production of documents (set two) is GRANTED. The discovery requests at issue are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (See CCP 2017.010.) Plaintiff's "other vehicle" requests regarding the same alleged defects are proper. (See Donlen v. Ford Motor Co. (2013) 217 Cal.App.4th 138, 154 [explaining probative value of "other vehicle" evidence]; Santana v. FCA US, LLC (2020) 56 Cal.App.5th 334, 346-347 [noting intentionally inadequate "repair" of fuel pump based on defendant's prior knowledge].) Defendant American Honda Motor Co., Inc.'s objections (e.g., vague, overbroad) lack merit and are otherwise unsupported. Defendant shall produce all non-privileged requested documents by January 30, 2025. If there are privileged documents, a privilege log should be produced. Defendant presents no competent evidence showing that the requests are unduly burdensome. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: **Transferred to Contra Costa County** MARIANA GREUB ET AL VS. AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents Set Two From Defendant American Honda Motor Co., And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Tuesday, January 7, 2025, Line 3. 2 - PLAINTIFFs MARIANA GREUB, and ESTON GREUB's Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents Set Two From Defendant American Honda Motor Co., And Request For Sanctions. Plaintiffs Mariana and Eston Greub's Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents Set Two From Defendant American Honda Motor Co., And Request For Sanctions to which no opposition was filed is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. The notice is based on the wrong statute, as is the memorandum of points and authorities. The motion is denied without prejudice to the filing of proper notice and a properly grounded memorandum.
